articles.title: New algorithm identifies disease-linked changes in cells without prior training
articles.summary: A new algorithm could drive breakthroughs in understanding cancer, Alzheimer's disease and other potentially fatal conditions. Researchers from the University of Waterloo developed the machine-learning algorithm, called RNovA, to detect changes in the proteins in human cells. Their research is published in the journal Nature Biotechnology.

articles.title: Unique instruments automate sample preparation, quality control for cryo-electron microscopy
articles.summary: Cryo-electron microscopy (cryo-EM) can help scientists determine the three-dimensional structure of proteins in unprecedented detail. Jacques Dubochet, former group leader at EMBL, shared the 2017 Nobel Prize in chemistry with Joachim Frank and Richard Henderson for the development of this technique, which led to the "resolution revolution" in structural biology.

articles.title: Researchers provide economic evidence base for shift away from animal testing
articles.summary: Researchers at Northumbria University have produced new economic evidence demonstrating the significant and growing commercial value of alternatives to animal testing in the U.K., with findings already featuring in parliamentary debate on the issue.

articles.title: New energy-boosting quantum mechanism discovered in photosynthetic bacteria
articles.summary: Researchers have discovered how certain photosynthetic bacteria use a sophisticated quantum mechanism to increase their efficiency when capturing sunlight. The study, published today in the journal Nature Chemistry and led by Professor Jenny Clark, reveals that nature has been using a process called "singlet fission," effectively a "two-for-one" energy deal, to optimize solar harvesting. The findings provide a new blueprint for green technology, particularly as engineers attempt to copy this mechanism to build next-generation solar panels and quantum technologies.

articles.title: Can scientists learn cells' language? Researchers aim to decode cellular conversations
articles.summary: Multicellular life depends on remarkable acts of cooperation. Every cell in the human body must sense what is happening around it, interpret signals from its neighbors and respond in ways that support the larger tissue. These chemical, physical and electrical messages help determine when cells grow, repair damage, fight infection or quiet down after a threat has passed—and tissue health depends on how well those parts come together.

articles.title: New AI tool identifies wild animals by their unique patterns in real time
articles.summary: Patterns reveal the individual: A novel AI algorithm equips researchers with a powerful new tool to accurately identify individual jaguars, zebras and giraffes in real time based on their unique coat patterns. Biologists and ecologists can now observe individual wild animals as they move from place to place, as well as their behavior and development over long periods, accurately, with much less effort and significantly faster than before—a major step forward for nature conservation. The scientists explain how this works in the journal Methods in Ecology and Evolution.

articles.title: Amazon fish reveal a synchronized survival tactic that could transfer to drone swarms
articles.summary: Some fish swim in synchrony. Others, it turns out, breathe in synchrony. This is true for arapaimas, an obligate air-breathing species living in the Amazon. A new study in Communications Biology, led by the Leibniz Institute of Freshwater Ecology and Inland Fisheries (IGB) in collaboration with the Cluster of Excellence "Science of Intelligence," has demonstrated for the first time that arapaima juveniles gather by the hundreds to synchronize their trips to the water surface with split-second precision, most likely to avoid predators and maximize survival and efficiency.

articles.title: 'Super fungi' offer greener path to recovery of critical minerals
articles.summary: A "superpowered" fungus engineered at The University of Queensland could be used to extract critical minerals from toxic mining waste while also helping to remediate sites. Environmental engineers at UQ's new Biosustainability Hub are growing the unique fungal strains that can be used to detoxify mining tailings and capture traces of important rare earths without the need for harsh chemicals.

articles.title: First quantum biosensor can detect rapid, invisible changes in cells
articles.summary: In the development of diseases such as muscular dystrophy, cancer, Ebola and dengue, numerous chemical reactions take place within and between cells that contribute to disease progression. These changes can occur in less than the blink of an eye—within microseconds or faster—making them difficult to analyze. Quantum sensing offers a way to monitor these changes, helping researchers determine when, where and how rapid biological processes occur.

articles.title: Mapping and engineering the human cell–cell interactome
articles.summary: Nature Biotechnology, Published online: 23 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41587-026-03177-2 Di Carlo and colleagues discuss technologies required to map and engineer the human cell–cell interactome and the therapeutic avenues such an atlas could unlock.

articles.title: Congress sends anti-CBDC housing bill to Trump’s desk for final approval
articles.summary: The bill, which seeks to boost housing affordability in America, also includes language banning CBDC until Dec. 31, 2030.

articles.title: Ethereum’s much-hated staking 'tax' may already be obsolete
articles.summary: Ethereum’s latest “funding crisis” has triggered a fierce debate over whether to tax staking rewards or to pursue funding from large ETH holders for new organizations like EthLabs.

articles.title: AI chipmaker Cerebras down 11% after first public earnings report
articles.summary: While revenue was up 92% from year-ago levels, the company forecasted a lower core gross margin next quarter.

articles.title: Starmer steps down: What Andy Burnham means for crypto in the UK
articles.summary: Burnham brings a dose of optimism to the UK crypto industry, having previously supported the blockchain industry as mayor of Manchester.

articles.title: Crypto critic Roubini joins tokenization boom with onchain 'Technodollar'
articles.summary: The economist known as "Dr. Doom" is supporting a tokenized security backed by a Nasdaq-listed ETF he oversees, designed to protect wealth in global crises.

articles.title: Senate Democrats urge Republicans to hold hearings on Trump family crypto ties to Abu Dhabi royalty
articles.summary: Top Democrats are calling for congressional hearings into President Donald Trump’s family crypto venture and Abu Dhabi royalty.

articles.title: Mark Zuckerberg ordered Meta staff to develop moneyless prediction market: NYT
articles.summary: The Meta CEO reportedly plans for the company to launch a prediction market independent of its other apps allowing users to place wagers using a points system rather than money.

articles.title: Trump directing ‘government dollars and time’ to quantum security could be a boon for bitcoin
articles.summary: Millions of bitcoins with exposed public addresses may be at risk to quantum computers, Project Eleven previously said.
